summary: Frontend PWA mobile-friendly testing for Radhya MFI HRMS. Tested mobile bottom nav (390px), desktop sidebar (1280px), modal z-index, punch button touch targets, More button, and login page. All primary mobile features working correctly.

integration_issues.issue: Returns 401 - no user_account linked to employee RMF0001 in the DB. test_credentials.md lists RMF0001 but user account is empty {}. Pre-existing issue from iteration 6.

design_issues.screen: Modal + Made with Emergent badge on mobile
issues: Badge z-index is platform-injected at 9999, which is above modal z-[70]=70. Badge appears in bottom-right corner even when modal backdrop is open. However, in practice the badge (small, bottom-right) does NOT block modal interactive elements (centered dialog). The badge's CSS fix (bottom:64px) correctly prevents it from overlapping the bottom nav. This is an acceptable platform constraint.
Review request states 'Modal dialogs use z-[70] so they appear above badge' - technically incorrect (badge z-9999 > modal z-70), but the intended fix (modal above bottom nav z-60) works correctly.

action_items: Optional: If badge truly needs to be behind modals, add CSS to index.css overriding badge z-index: `body > div:not(#root), body > a:not(#root) { z-index: 65 !important; }` — this would put badge below modal (70) but above bottom nav (60). WARNING: This may break platform functionality.
Optional: Create user account for RMF0001 so employee test credentials work (Admin -> Employees -> RMF0001 -> Create Login Account with password Welcome@123)
critical_code_review_comments: z-index stacking: Bottom Nav z-60, Modal z-70, Sidebar Overlay z-80, Made with Emergent badge z-9999. Badge is always on top due to platform injection. Current implementation is correct for its stated purpose (modal above bottom nav).
Mobile nav items for admin: Home/Attend/Leaves/Team/More (4 items + More). For employee role (no /employees access): Home/Attend/Leaves/Payroll/More - code logic is correct per MOBILE_NAV_PRIORITY.
CameraCapture component (Attendance.js) uses z-[70] (line 57), correctly aligned with other modals.
Safe-area CSS for iPhone X+ (env safe-area-inset-bottom) is implemented correctly in both index.css and Layout.js bottom nav style attribute.
test_credentials: admin/Admin@123 works. RMF0001/Welcome@123 returns 401 (no user account linked).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All mobile PWA features validated: bottom nav visible at 390px (Home/Attend/Leaves/Team/More for admin), desktop sidebar visible at 1280px with bottom nav hidden, pb-24 on main content, py-4 punch buttons, More button opens sidebar overlay. Employee login broken (no user account for RMF0001) - pre-existing issue. Badge z-index (9999) technically above modals (70) but doesn't block modal interactions in practice.
rca_of_the_issue: NA - all primary features working as expected. The only note is the badge z-index (9999) is platform-injected and cannot easily be overridden without risking platform side effects.
